answer:
The Aircall API supports two authentication methods: HTTP Basic Auth and OAuth2 [1][2].

Use Basic Auth if you are an Aircall customer building for your own account [1]. Generate your API ID and API Token via the Aircall Dashboard under Integrations & API [3][4]. Authenticate by setting the Authorization header to a Base64-encoded string of your credentials [2][5]:

```bash
Authorization: Basic <base64(api_id:api_token)>
```

Use OAuth2 for integrations intended for multiple customers, such as those listed in the Aircall App Marketplace [1]. This method utilizes a Bearer token for authorization [2].
